root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Web Client Reference Manual
root.summary: Front matter and full contents of the Cúram 8.1.2 Web Client Reference Manual. This section includes the edition and legal notices, then indexes the entire guide: web client architecture and UIM metadata, application configuration and Smart Navigator, sections/tabs/navigation, deployment and customization, localization, session/browser management, autorecovery, domain-specific controls, custom conversion/sorting, online help, dynamic UIM maintenance and limitations, UI test automation with data-testid, and the full UIM reference. It also notes Dynamic UIM initialization behavior, including the `dynamicUIMInitModelOnStart` setting. Useful as the entry point to the manual rather than for a single technical topic.

children.title: Note
children.summary: This note tells readers to review the Notices section on page 397 before using the document or the product it supports. It also includes the Merative US L.P. copyright notice for 2012 and 2024.

children.title: Edition
Edition — pages 5-14
children.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 guide: edition notice, copyright/trademark/legal pages, and the table of contents. The TOC shows the document covers web client architecture, deployment and customization, application configuration, Smart Navigator, sections/tabs/navigation, case and participant pages, coding standards, online help, dynamic UIM maintenance, UI test automation, and a detailed UIM reference with element/widget documentation. Useful as an entry point to see the full scope of the manual rather than a technical topic section.

children.title: Edition — pages 15-24
children.summary: Introduces the Cúram web client and its CDEJ concepts for building standard web applications with Java EE, JSP/servlets, HTML, JavaScript, and CSS. Explains UIM XML pages, fields, clusters, lists, action controls, server interfaces, initialization vs process phases, and parameter mapping via CONNECT. Includes a sample UIM page and notes UIM must be well-formed XML. Also overviews application UI structure: banner, sections, tabs, context panel, shortcut panel, page title/actions, cluster and field display, smart panel, and standardized inline/overflow action menus.

children.title: Edition — pages 25-34
children.summary: Introduces the Cúram web client UI and application structure: common interface elements (tabs, lists, buttons, inputs, modal dialogs, wizard controls), Carbon-based components/add-ons, and how applications are assembled from .app, .sec, and .tab files. Explains page contexts (content area, context panel, list dropdown panel, modal dialog, smart panel), page layout limits, AppController.do and direct URL behavior, and the web client development environment and artifact/build process.

children.title: Edition — pages 35-44
children.summary: Overview of Cúram web client project structure and build setup. Covers installation folders (<app-dir>, <client-dir>, <server-dir>, <cdej-dir>, <sdej-dir>), CDEJ-recognized client directory layout, component-based customization and component order via CLIENT_COMPONENT_ORDER, locale handling via LOCALE_LIST, currency symbol/placement configuration, and Ant build targets (client, clean, beandoc, client-with-previews, uimgen) plus required environment variables. Useful for navigating web client development, localization, and deployment/build topics.

children.title: Edition — pages 45-54
children.summary: Covers web client build and runtime setup. Explains client build properties, full vs incremental builds, clean builds after upgrades, headless ANT_OPTS, dependency checking, build logs, and validation error reporting. Describes generating server interface reference docs and static page previews, including domain-default defaults. Lists key client properties for dates, uploads, synchronizer tokens, tab session persistence, progress spinner, error pages, DBtoJMS, caching, Dynamic UIM init, link sanitization, and tracing. Also explains customizing web.xml across environments and locale/HTTPS/error-page settings.

children.title: Edition — pages 55-64
children.summary: Covers Cúram web client customization across pages 55–64: default error page behavior, component order, adding/overriding/merging artifacts, externalized strings, images and image mappings, CuramLinks.properties, runtime XML config files, JavaScript/CSS customization, application-specific CSS, and application configuration files. Also explains curam-config.xml merge rules, overriding/dividing address configuration, configuration file naming conventions, and introduces mandatory STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER setup for Kubernetes/static content deployment.

children.title: Edition — pages 65-74
children.summary: Cúram web client configuration for static content, error handling, UI behavior, and deployment settings. Covers setting STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER, packaging and relocating static files to an HTTP server with cache headers, and notes Kubernetes deployment requirements. Also documents ERROR_PAGE, MULTIPLE_SELECT, ADMIN cache updates, security checks on page load, select-all checkboxes, transfer lists, conditional links, autocomplete, scrollbars, pagination, and custom WebContent resource overrides. Ends with deployment notes and ApplicationConfiguration.properties date/time settings.

children.title: Edition — pages 75-84
children.summary: This section covers web client customization and configuration. It documents many CDEJ property settings (uploads, synchronizer tokens, tab sessions, progress spinner, error pages, resource cache, UIM startup, link sanitization), web.xml customization and HTTPS settings, tracing server calls, and how artifacts are added, overridden, or merged across components. It also explains externalized strings, image properties and mappings, CuramLinks.properties, runtime XML configs, login page customization, and JavaScript file usage and caveats.

children.title: Edition — pages 85-94
children.summary: Cúram web client customization and global UI configuration. Covers supported JavaScript/CSS extension practices, custom CSS loading and application-specific styling, and cautions about undocumented HTML/CSS and third-party APIs. Explains application config file placement/merging rules, curam-config.xml override/merge behavior, and key settings including ERROR_PAGE, MULTIPLE_SELECT, ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS, APPEND_COLON, ADMIN cache updates, STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER static asset packaging/cache headers, FIELD_ERROR_INDICATOR, SECURITY_CHECK_ON_PAGE_LOAD, ENABLE_SELECT_ALL_CHECKBOX, TRANSFER_LISTS_MODE, HIDE_CONDITIONAL_LINKS, DISABLE_AUTO_COMPLETE, SCROLLBAR_CONFIG, and PAGINATION.

children.title: Edition — pages 95-104
children.summary: Covers web client configuration for pagination and custom resources, then introduces application configuration. Explains how to add WebContent files, the risks of overwriting generated resources, and build-time copying behavior. Describes application config files (.app, .sec, .ssp, .tab, .nav, .mnu) and validation/processing. Details web client properties for browser tab title. Begins application definitions: banner elements, section refs, menu/logout/preferences, logos, context, and application search setup including single-search, multi-search, smart navigator notes, and advanced search links.

children.title: Edition — pages 105-114
children.summary: Cúram application configuration for Smart Navigator and related app settings. Covers section-ref limits, timeout-warning dialog attributes, application context setup via .app files and ApplicationContext code tables, context-aware code table views, optional custom headers, a sample application and login mapping via APPLICATION_CODE, and how to enable/disable Smart Navigator. Also explains creating search targets and keywords, including administration steps and CT_SearchTarget/SearchTargetKeyword hierarchy and binding rules.

children.title: Edition — pages 115-124
children.summary: This section explains how to configure Cúram Smart Navigator search targets and keywords, including creating target implementations, binding code table entries to Java classes with Guice, and adding module classes to MODULECLASSNAME.dmx. It also covers deleting, disabling, modifying, and translating keywords/targets, plus rebuilding the database after XML or module changes. The latter pages describe overriding the person search and customizing case search filtering/results via custom or extended SearchResultFilter implementations and related Guice bindings.

children.title: Edition — pages 125-134
children.summary: Covers Smart Navigator configuration and section setup. Includes adding custom Guice bindings and module classes (.dmx) for custom implementations, overriding case search result descriptions via CaseTypeDescriptionResolver, changing search target redirect URLs and preferred tabs, configuring recent-search history and debounce timeout, and implementing a JavaScript pre-navigation hook. Also introduces application sections: .sec section definitions, section tabs, optional shortcut panels (.ssp), and node types (group/leaf/separator) used to organize shortcuts.

children.title: Edition — pages 135-144
children.summary: Covers application configuration for section shortcut panels, tabs, and tab actions menus. Includes XML file formats and key attributes for shortcut nodes, tab-config, context, navigation, smart-panel, tab-refresh, and menu-bar. Explains how tabs use page parameters, context panels, navigation bars, smart panels, and refresh events, plus restrictions on context/smart panel UIM pages. Also gives example .ssp and .tab files and notes corresponding .properties files.

children.title: Edition — pages 145-154
children.summary: Cúram application configuration for tab actions menus and tab navigation. Covers menu-bar structure, menu-item/submenu/menu-separator/loader-registry elements, modal dialogs, file downloads, inline tab actions, and dynamic enable/disable or show/hide behavior via loaders. Includes XML examples and properties-file usage. Also defines .nav navigation files: navigation, nodes, navigation-group, navigation-page, and loader-registry/loader elements for tab and page-group navigation, including dynamic state support and remembering selected page within a tab.

children.title: Edition — pages 155-164
children.summary: Cúram application configuration for tab navigation and actions menus. Covers dynamic navigation support via .nav dynamic="true", loader-registry, and DynamicNavStateLoader; unique identifiers and a loadNavState method to enable/disable or hide tabs/pages. Explains opening sections/tabs from controls or links, page-to-tab/tab-to-section associations, parameter-based tab instances, limitations, and sequential tab ordering. Also documents standardized tab/page/list actions menus, inline vs overflow behavior, best practices, and how to enable/configure inline items with application properties.

children.title: Edition — pages 165-174
children.summary: Covers application configuration for overriding inline action counts on tab, page, and list menus via max-inline-items/MAX_INLINE_ITEMS, with examples and build note. Introduces localization: number formatting, file encodings (XML, Java properties, non-XML), locale naming and LOCALE_LIST, language toggle for internal users, and localizable text translations. Explains short vs rich text entity settings, translation storage, locale fallback order, migrated-legacy field fallback, and admin editing of translations.

children.title: Edition — pages 175-184
children.summary: Covers localization and session management. Explains externalized strings for UIM, JavaScript, images, infrastructure widgets, CDEJResources, ApplicationConfiguration, runtime messages, application menu, and tabbed artifacts, including property-file naming, merging/override rules, placeholders, and customization/localization procedures. Also describes browser sessions (30-minute inactivity timeout, logout/browser restart behavior), max open tabs per section, tab-restoration behavior across refresh vs new session, and related customization points for dialogs and button labels.

children.title: Edition — pages 185-194
children.summary: Covers Cúram session and browser management. Explains tab session persistence settings, when tab data is written to DB, logout behavior, and risks when users share logins or browser sessions. Details session timeout warnings: default texts, modal size, buffering, and how to customize them globally or per application, including custom logon pages. Also notes tab-session record limits and browser-specific session issues. Begins browser Back/Refresh/Close button behavior, with warnings, admin property control, and browser/tablet quirks.

children.title: Edition — pages 195-204
children.summary: Browser management covers guarding against leaving internal/external apps and optimal browser support. It explains configurable properties for browser-version detection, check frequency, and message text/icons in ApplicationConfiguration.properties. Autorecovery describes restoring unsaved modal form data after tab refresh/close or session timeout, with global enablement, throttling, supported UIM modals and fields, and unsupported cases like file upload, passwords, radio buttons, nested modals, VPN/network-loss failures, and multiple browsers/incognito. It also lists production setup, session/database behavior, and cache-clear implications.

children.title: Edition — pages 205-214
children.summary: Covers Cúram “domain-specific controls” for richer UIs: date, three-field date, date-time, time-only, frequency pattern selector, selection lists, transfer lists, user preferences editor, and rules trees. Explains how domains map to widgets, JavaScript/CSS dependencies, configurable formats and defaults, multi-select setup, code table/server-populated lists, and rules tree modes (default, summary, failure, dynamic) with XML config options for icons, hyperlinks, open-node behavior, and decision-ID sourcing.

children.title: Edition — pages 215-224
children.summary: Covers Cúram domain-specific controls for rules and charts. Explains dynamic rules view filtering behavior, DYNAMIC_FULL_TREE, and RULES_DEFINITION editor configuration via RulesEditorConfig.xml, including hyperlink text, open-node, and decision-ID sourcing. Describes meeting view for USER_DAILY_SCHEDULE, single/multiple selection, XML format, and scheduling hyperlink behavior. Also documents chart configuration (ChartConfig.xml/BarChartConfig.xml), legend/link/caption-link settings, axes, captions, tooltips, localization, and system administration steps for changing chart colors.

children.title: Edition — pages 225-234
children.summary: Cúram 8.1.2 domain-specific controls: how to customize chart colors (bar charts and Participation Summary), XML data formats for charts, Heatmap widget setup/configuration, Workflow view XML and WorkflowConfig.xml settings, and Evidence view display/comparison modes with properties-based configuration. Includes key XML schemas, attributes, icons, hyperlinks, localization, and visibility/link settings for each control.

children.title: Edition — pages 235-244
children.summary: Covers Cúram domain-specific UI controls and their XML/configuration formats: Evidence View compare/display XML schemas; Calendar data and CalendarConfig.xml (event/single-day event schemas, view types, icons, links, locale config); Payment Statement view domains and properties; Batch Function view; ADDRESS_DATA and locale-specific address formats/mandatory fields; Schedule view UIM setup and bitfield encoding; radio button groups for code tables; and pop-up/multi-popup page behavior.

children.title: Edition — pages 245-254
children.summary: Covers Cúram domain-specific controls for pop-up and Agenda Player configuration. Explains how to define single and multiple pop-up pages in curam-config.xml, including domain/CT_CODE mapping, images/labels, sizing, editable text input behavior, and how UIM pop-up pages return value/description pairs via DISMISS or SUBMIT_AND_DISMISS. Also shows how fields invoke pop-ups and pass parameters. Introduces Agenda Player layout, navigation modes (basic/incremental/full), claimant view, and AgendaConfig.xml player settings plus localization/customization files.

children.title: Edition — pages 255-264
children.summary: This section covers Agenda Player customization and configuration: property files for progress bar text, button labels, frame titles, and quit dialog text; required UIM structures for navigation, summary, and exit pages; agenda XML page-flow attributes like initial, disableback/forward, submitonnext, and close-on-submit; status-to-image mapping via ImageMapConfig.xml; and conditional display using CONTEXT/inWizard. It then introduces the LOCALIZED_MESSAGE domain, followed by the start of custom data conversion and sorting, including format/parse/validate/compare operations, lifecycle, and domain plug-ins.

children.title: Edition — pages 265-274
children.summary: Introduces Cúram domain plug-ins for custom data conversion and sorting: converter, comparator, default, and options plug-ins, plus their inheritance/configuration model. Explains the DomainPlugIn, DomainConverter, ClientDomainConverter, DomainComparator, and DomainDefault interfaces, including pre-parse/pre-validate, generic parse/format, and default-value behavior. Describes XML configuration and inheritance across domains. Lists provided abstract/base classes and built-in converter plug-ins, with formatting/parse behavior for major SVR types, dates/times, money, strings, localized messages, and code-table values.

children.title: Edition — pages 275-284
children.summary: Covers Cúram domain plug-ins for custom data conversion, sorting, default values, and error reporting. Includes parser and pre-validate behavior by domain, comparator plug-ins for locale-aware and case-insensitive sorting, collation strength configuration, and default value mappings. Explains infrastructure vs conversion/domain exceptions, how to create custom ConversionException classes and message catalogs, numeric message IDs, and exception chaining. Also lists Java object representations for root domains and gives customization/configuration guidelines for plug-ins and runtime classpath placement.

children.title: Edition — pages 285-294
children.summary: Covers custom domain plug-ins for data conversion and sorting: overriding converter format, parse, and validate methods; examples for currency symbols, year formatting, even-number validation, and custom error messages for pattern failures. Explains custom comparators for sort order, including placing zero dates last and locale-aware comparison, plus cautions about pre-validation side effects. Also describes custom default value plug-ins, including dynamically computed defaults, and notes type/null checks are handled by client infrastructure.

children.title: Edition — pages 295-304
children.summary: Covers custom data conversion/sorting plug-in rules, naming conventions, generic parsing, and code-table limitations. Key guidance: domain plug-ins are stateful by domain/locale, use pooled instances, must not store custom fields, and need default constructors. Utility methods should use underscore prefixes; avoid package names starting with curam. Explains parseGeneric behavior and when to override it. Notes code-table plug-in customization is unsupported. Also introduces online help development and dynamic UIM pages, including property-file help entries, localization, and adding dynamic UIM/resources via AppResource.dmx.

children.title: Edition — pages 305-314
children.summary: Cúram documentation covering maintenance and publishing of dynamic UIM pages in the Resource Store, including search, upload, edit, delete, validate, category/locale handling, and runtime publication. Also introduces UI test automation with the data-testid attribute, its naming format, configuration via custom renderers, and benefits over brittle IDs/titles/CSS paths. Begins the UIM reference: defines UIM as XML translated to JSP, distinguishes PAGE and VIEW documents, explains page/view inclusion rules, externalized strings, field validations, and the reference conventions for elements and attributes.

children.title: Edition — pages 315-324
children.summary: Explains UIM page connections and layout/action elements. Covers connection types SOURCE, TARGET, INITIAL and endpoint NAME values (server interface, TEXT, CONSTANT, PAGE, JSCRIPT_REF), with examples of page parameters and server-interface wiring. Describes ACTION_CONTROL types (ACTION, SUBMIT, FILE_DOWNLOAD, CLIPBOARD, DISMISS, SUBMIT_AND_DISMISS), attributes like LABEL, TYPE, DEFAULT, ACTION_ID, IMAGE, ALIGNMENT, and child LINK/CONNECT/SCRIPT/CONDITION. Also covers ACTION_SET layout/options (TOP/BOTTOM, ALIGNMENT, TYPE, inline menu behavior, separators) and CLUSTER attributes for page layout (TITLE, NUM_COLS, TAB_ORDER, SHOW_LABELS).

children.title: Edition — pages 325-334
children.summary: This section documents UIM elements for clusters and related layout/behavior: CLUSTER attributes like LAYOUT_ORDER, WIDTH, STYLE, DESCRIPTION, LABEL_WIDTH, BEHAVIOR/EXPANDED, SUMMARY, and SCROLL_HEIGHT; cluster child elements and nesting; dynamic conditional clusters using CONDITION, SCRIPT, CONTROL_REF, and CONNECT with JavaScript; restrictions and supported data types; the CONNECT and CONTAINER elements; DETAILS_ROW for expandable list rows; and the DESCRIPTION element for building titles/descriptions from sources. Useful if you need cluster layout, conditional display, or list expansion behavior.

children.title: Edition — pages 335-344
children.summary: Cúram UIM reference covering dropdown behavior and several view elements. It compares code-table hierarchy dropdowns vs Carbon Combo box behavior, including opening, filtering, clear button, selection, and highlighting. It then documents FIELD attributes and child elements, including connections, control modes, initial focus, blank values, and dynamic conditional clusters. Also covers FOOTER_ROW, IMAGE, INCLUDE, INITIAL, and INFORMATIONAL elements with their attributes, usage, and constraints. Useful for implementation details of fields, lists, pop-ups, included UIMs, and server-returned messages.

children.title: Edition — pages 345-354
children.summary: Covers UIM page/navigation elements and link behavior. Introduces INLINE_PAGE for inlining another UIM page in expandable list rows, with restrictions (view-only, no complex widgets, no editing, no circular dependencies). Explains IS_TRUE/IS_FALSE boolean tests for server interface properties, JSP_SCRIPTLET usage mainly for redirects, and LABEL for concatenating values. Details LINK attributes for internal/external pages, URIs, modal dialogs, return-page behavior, and parameters via CONNECT, plus notes on modal dialog usage and limitations.

children.title: Edition — pages 355-364
children.summary: Covers UIM modal dialog options and custom JS control, including draggable modals. Then details the LIST element: attributes, child elements, sorting, pagination, collapsible behavior, search-page row counts, and editable list rules/allowed data types. Explains LIST_CONNECT for hidden row data submission. Also introduces MENU types: static, navigation, dynamic, integrated case, in-page navigation, and wizard progress bar, including their XML structure, configuration files, and wizard page/resource requirements.

children.title: Edition — pages 365-374
children.summary: Cúram UIM reference for page-level document structure and related elements. Covers PAGE attributes (PAGE_ID, POPUP_PAGE, SCRIPT_FILE, APPEND_COLON, WINDOW_OPTIONS, TYPE, HIDE_CONDITIONAL_LINKS), child elements (INCLUDE, PAGE_TITLE, DESCRIPTION, MENU, SERVER_INTERFACE, CLUSTER, LIST, SCRIPT, etc.), and page parameters. Also defines PAGE_TITLE/TAB_NAME/TITLE construction, SCRIPT events/actions, SERVER_INTERFACE phases and ACTION_ID_PROPERTY, plus SOURCE and TARGET endpoints for data connections. Useful for understanding how UIM pages, titles, scripts, and server calls are configured.

children.title: Edition — pages 375-384
children.summary: Covers the UIM `VIEW` root element and the UIM widgets reference. Defines `WIDGET` and `WIDGET_PARAMETER` basics, then details `FILE_EDIT` (Word editing on Windows/Chrome/Edge, required DOCUMENT/DETAILS parameters, native messaging config), `FILE_UPLOAD` (browser file picker, upload size/path config, CONTENT/FILE_NAME/CONTENT_TYPE/ACCEPTABLE_CONTENT_TYPES), `FILE_DOWNLOAD` (link generation and servlet/page-parameter wiring), and `MULTISELECT` (row checkboxes, select-all, delimited selection data).

children.title: Edition — pages 385-394
children.summary: Covers UIM list selection widgets and dynamic UIM support limits. Explains MULTISELECT usage in a LIST, required parameters, delimited row-key strings, confirmation-page behavior, and lack of pagination support; also introduces SINGLESELECT parameters. Then defines dynamic UIM (resource-store cached, PAGE as the only root, strings externalized) and lists unsupported features by element for dynamic UIM, including many ACTION_CONTROL, ACTION_SET, CLUSTER, CONTAINER, FIELD, LINK, LIST, MENU, PAGE, PAGE_TITLE, SERVER_INTERFACE, and WIDGET options.

children.title: Edition — pages 395-396
children.summary: Describes Dynamic UIM system initialization. It explains the system can start at application launch or be deferred until the first Dynamic UIM page request, with launch-time initialization as the default. To defer initialization, set `dynamicUIMInitModelOnStart=false` in `ApplicationConfiguration.properties`. Use the default if the application accesses Dynamic UIM pages; override it only when Dynamic UIM is unused and Tomcat startup time needs improvement.

Front matter and the full Cúram 8.1.2 web client manual scope. It includes edition/copyright pages, TOC, and coverage of web client architecture and UI metadata, application/section/tab/navigation definitions, deployment and customization, application configuration, Smart Navigator, UI widgets and UIM reference, dynamic UIM maintenance and limitations, and UI test automation via data-testid. Also notes dynamic UIM initialization behavior and the `dynamicUIMInitModelOnStart` setting. Useful as an entry point to the whole guide rather than a single topic.

children.title: Contents
children.summary: This node is the table of contents for the Cúram 8.1.2 Web Client Reference. It lists the major chapters and subtopics: web client overview, application configuration, navigation (smart navigator, sections, tabs, tab actions and menus), page and UI customization, dynamic UIM maintenance, UI test automation, and a detailed UIM reference with elements, widgets, unsupported dynamic UIM features, and system initialization. Useful for locating specific topics in the manual rather than containing substantive content itself.

children.title: 1 Web client reference
children.summary: Introduces the Web client reference for developing a standard Cúram web client. Describes the client as an HTML user interface generated by a middle-tier web application, aligned with Java EE architecture and driven by JSP and servlet technology. Notes that the UI uses standard browser and Web 2.0 technologies such as JavaScript and CSS.

children.title: 2 Web client overview
2.1 User interface metadata (UIM)
Page content metadata
children.summary: Introduces the Cúram web client and User Interface Metadata (UIM). Explains that UIM is XML used to define page contents and limited layout for consistent UI generation, with .uim files mapping fields, labels, buttons, links, clusters, lists, and actions to server interface parameters. Covers page content metadata, CONNECT mappings, externalized static text, page parameters, and SERVER_INTERFACE methods for initialization and submit/process phases. Includes an example UIM extract for a person search page showing PAGE, CLUSTER, LIST, and ACTION_CONTROL usage.

Explains User Interface Metadata (UIM), the XML used to define Cúram web client pages. UIM files (.uim) describe page contents and limited layout, with standard elements like PAGE, FIELD, CLUSTER, LIST, ACTION_CONTROL, and CONNECT mapping UI fields to server interface parameters. Covers externalized static text, page parameters, and SERVER_INTERFACE methods for initialization and submit/process phases. Includes an example person search page showing page title, search clusters, submit/reset actions, and results list.

children.title: 2.2 Application user interface overview
2.2 Application user interface overview — pages 20-29
children.summary: Overview of Cúram web client UI structure and UIM-based page specification. Defines main application elements: banner, search, welcome message, menu, sections, tabs, context/shortcut panels, page navigation, titles, action menus, refresh/print/help controls, clusters, lists, labels, fields, smart panel, and action controls. Also illustrates common component types such as date/time inputs, search pop-up, text area, buttons, modal dialogs, wizard/progress indicator, and registration/search page layout. Useful for understanding application page composition and UI component behavior.

children.title: 2.2 Application user interface overview — pages 30-30
children.summary: This section overviews Cúram application UI elements shown in a wizard modal, including modal title, help and close actions, progress indicator, page description, text input, checkbox, date input, and dropdown. It also introduces Carbon Design System alignment, listing UIM components updated to Carbon equivalents (for example, checkbox, date/time, modal dialog, text area, time input) and noting a new Carbon add-on called Cluster.

Overview of Cúram 8.1.2 web client UI metadata (UIM) and page composition. Explains that UIM XML defines application pages and describes core UI structure: banner, search, welcome, menu, sections/tabs, context panels, shortcut panels, page navigation, titles, actions, refresh/print/help controls, clusters, lists, labels, fields, smart panel, and action controls. Includes examples of common widgets such as date/time inputs, search pop-ups, text areas, buttons, lists, and wizard/modal flows. Also notes Carbon Design System updates and the Cluster add-on.

children.title: 2.3 Cúram applications
children.summary: This section explains what a Cúram application is: a role-specific user interface made up mostly of UIM pages. It describes how an application controls screen layout and navigation flow, including links from section shortcuts, tab navigation, page group navigation, and page content. It also notes that navigation opens pages in the correct tab/section context or in modal dialogs. Applications are defined in XML using .app files, with referenced sections in .sec files and tabs in .tab files.

children.title: 2.4 Page context
children.summary: This section explains how UIM pages behave differently depending on the application context. It introduces page context as a factor that can change the behavior of some page elements, and notes the main context shown here is the Content Area. It follows an example application configuration that defines an application banner, quick search, menu items, and two section references, illustrating how multiple reusable sections can be assembled in an application.

children.title: 2.5 Page appearance
children.summary: This section explains page appearance limitations in the web client. It says application/page metadata only gives limited control over UI layout: the banner, sections, and tabs are fixed; clusters and lists flow top to bottom; and fields are auto-positioned. It notes that defaults reduce the need for layout attributes, and page-specific actions can be configured with UIM ACTION_SET and ACTION_CONTROL. It also points to page action menus for further details.

children.title: 2.6 The application controller JSP and web client URL
children.summary: This section explains that the Cúram web client is rendered by a single JSP file, AppController.do, and that the browser URL always ends with AppController.do and does not change as users navigate. It also sits within the web client overview, following descriptions of content area behavior, page appearance, and page-specific actions, so it is useful for understanding how the client is launched and how its URL is structured.

children.title: 2.7 Web client development environment
Outline of the client development process
children.summary: Describes the web client development environment and a typical client development workflow. Covers how CDEJ converts UIM files into JSPs, the role of supporting properties/configuration files, and how the client application is organized into functional components. It also notes that more complex pages can use Cúram UI Add-ons with JavaScript and IBM Carbon Design System. The outlined process starts with installing the development environment, which creates CDEJ/SDEJ projects, then creating or customizing source files.

children.title: The Cúram application and CDEJ installation folders
children.summary: Explains the Cúram application layout and install folders for development. The application has two main parts: a server application for business entities/logic and a web client for user presentation. It defines the typical folder structure and names: <app-dir>, <client-dir> (usually webclient), <server-dir> (usually EJBServer), <cdej-dir> (CuramCDEJ), and <sdej-dir> (CuramSDEJ). Gives an example installation at C:/Curam with corresponding paths.

children.title: CDEJ project folder structure
children.summary: Explains the CDEJ client project folder structure under <client-dir> and what each key folder is for. Covers build artifacts and bean-doc, buildlogs, components/core vs custom components, custom Images/javasource/WebContent deployment, JavaSource configuration, project deployment descriptor customization, and WebContent output including locale JSPs, previews, and WEB-INF internals. Also notes that WEB-INF contents are generated/copied during build and should not be modified, and mentions the server project has a similar component structure.

children.title: Client application component folders
children.summary: Explains how Cúram web client applications are organized into components under `<client-dir>/components`, with the core component as the default lowest-priority layer. Describes components as customization namespaces that can override core artifacts without editing core files, and notes that folder structure is mostly ignored except for unique artifact filenames. Covers the special `WebContent` folder exception, where duplicate filenames are allowed. Also explains component precedence via the `CLIENT_COMPONENT_ORDER` comma-separated environment variable, with highest priority first and core implicit last.

children.title: Client application component order
children.summary: Client application components are organized under `<client-dir>/components`, with the core component always present and never edited directly. Components act as customization layers, using unique artifact filenames within each component; folder structure is mostly irrelevant except for `WebContent`, which preserves paths. This section explains component order: `CLIENT_COMPONENT_ORDER` defines priority for same-named artifacts across components, as a comma-separated list with highest priority first. The core component is implicitly lowest priority and need not be listed.

children.title: Client application component artifacts
children.summary: Describes client application component artifacts and their priority within component order. Explains that a component’s artifacts share the same priority and can override or extend lower-priority components. Lists main artifact types: UIM pages (.uim) for web pages, UIM views (.vim) for reusable page sections, and properties files for language-specific text. Notes that properties files separate text from page structure for localization and do not support visual formatting.

children.title: Client application locales
Configuring the currency symbol, placement, and spacing
children.summary: Explains how administrators configure currency symbol display using application properties. Covers three settings: whether placement/spacing follow user locale or global properties, the symbol and optional non-breaking space, and whether the symbol appears before or after the amount. Notes these properties apply only to CURAM_AMOUNT fields on UIM pages; localized text in properties files and server-side messages must be handled at development time. Includes guidance for adding locale-specific currency formatting in English/French properties files.

Explains client application locales and how locale affects language, number/date formatting, collation, and currency display. Covers locale codes, JRE support, setting LOCALE_LIST, how it controls build output and available run-time language choices, and cautions about unsupported modules/admin users. Also introduces currency symbol placement/spacing configuration via application properties, including the three related properties, their scope to CURAM_AMOUNT fields, and the need to handle localized text and server-side messages at development time.

children.title: Building an application
Build targets
children.summary: Explains how to build Cúram client applications with Apache Ant targets. Shows how to run targets from the client directory, combine multiple targets, and gives examples like `build client` and `build clean client`. Lists available client project targets: `client`, `clean`, `beandoc`, `client-with-previews`, and `uimgen`, with brief purposes. Also introduces build-related environment variables, including required `CURAMCDEJ` and `CLIENT_DIR`, and notes the build command usually sets them for installed applications.

children.title: Related server build targets
children.summary: Explains server-side Ant targets needed by the client build. The client application’s configuration files live in the <server-dir> project and are processed by the inserttabconfiguration target, which combines and imports them into the database. The database target calls inserttabconfiguration as its final step. Also notes that the client build can run as a full build or, on later runs, an incremental build that updates only changed outputs.

children.title: Full and incremental builds
children.summary: Explains how the client build target produces the web client application as either a full build on first run or an incremental build on later runs by comparing source files to generated output and updating only changed artifacts. This section is useful for understanding build behavior, efficiency, and when a complete regeneration occurs.

children.title: Dependency checking
children.summary: Explains when incremental builds suffice versus when a full clean build is required. Notes that some changes, especially build-affecting curam-config.xml updates and certain server interface changes, are not detected automatically; changing interface properties is detected, but underlying domain-type changes like code-table name changes are not. Also mentions build logs location and that UIM/JSP generation validates XML well-formedness, schema/context rules, and externalized string references.

children.title: Build Logs
children.summary: Build logs are saved each time the client build runs in `<client-dir>/buildlogs`, with filenames based on the build start date and time. They help review build errors more easily than scrolling the console. The section also notes that incremental builds are usually sufficient, but a clean/full build is required after upgrades or when certain config or interface changes aren’t detected automatically. It further explains that UIM processing validates XML well-formedness, schema/context rules, and referenced externalized strings.

children.title: Error reporting
children.summary: The client build validates UIM files while generating JSPs. It checks that files are well-formed XML, do not include missing VIM files, conform to the UIM XML schema plus extra context-sensitive rules, and reference only externalized strings that exist in the associated properties files.

children.title: Server interface reference documentation
children.summary: Explains how to generate and use HTML reference docs for façade server interfaces in UIM. The beandoc target creates documentation in <client-dir>/build/bean-doc with class, operation, domain, and code-table cross-references, helping you choose valid property names. Notes that property names can use the shortest unique ending, and that the Copy button often doesn’t work in browsers. Also covers page previews via client-with-previews, which generate static preview pages in <client-dir>/WebContent/Previews without server interaction.

children.title: Page previews
children.summary: Explains how to generate and view page previews with the client-with-previews build target, which creates static HTML in <client-dir>/WebContent/Previews for the default locale. Preview pages can be opened directly in a browser without starting a server, show default field values, and do not support interaction. They display only the UIM-defined main content area, not headers, footers, or sidebars.

children.title: UIM Generator Tool
children.summary: Describes the UIM Generator tool for automatically creating a UIM page for a server interface. It explains how to start it from the <client-dir> folder using `build uimgen`, the initial need to locate `ServerAccessBeans.xml`, and the main UI elements: File menu, server-interface tree, Display Phase/Action Phase options, and Make Page button. It also outlines the generation workflow: select an interface, choose the phase, and save the generated UIM in the appropriate component folder.

children.title: External client applications
children.summary: Describes how to build and test external web client applications using the `external-client` target, which takes a `-Dapp` EAR name from `deployment_packaging.xml` and copies the selected components into `webclient/build/apps/<app name>` with Eclipse project files. Also outlines deployment-related client configuration, especially `ApplicationConfiguration.properties` in `curam/omega3`, including copying `Initial_ApplicationConfiguration.properties` and setting properties such as date format for the date selector widget.

Explains how to build a standard Cúram web client application with Apache Ant targets from `<client-dir>`, including `client`, `clean`, `beandoc`, `client-with-previews`, and `uimgen`. Covers required environment variables (`CURAMCDEJ`, `CLIENT_DIR`, `CLIENT_PROJECT_NAME`, `LOCALE_LIST`) and optional ones. Describes related server targets (`inserttabconfiguration`, `database`), full vs incremental builds, when a clean build is needed, build logs, validation/error reporting for UIM/JSP generation, HTML server-interface reference docs, static page previews, and the UIM Generator workflow.

children.title: Deployment
Application configuration properties
Tracing server function calls
children.summary: This section explains how to trace server function calls on the web tier by adding `TraceOn=true` to `CDEJResources.properties`. When enabled, all server function call inputs and outputs are written to Standard Out. It also notes that the web tier does not have a configurable Log4j 2 logging setup like the server tier due to classloader issues.

Describes `ApplicationConfiguration.properties` for web client deployment, including file location and copying the initial sample for new applications. Lists configurable settings for date/time formats, address defaults, file upload limits and temp storage, synchronizer token behavior, tab session persistence thresholds, progress spinner enablement/timeout, and other application flags such as stack trace output, DBtoJMS credentials, resource cache size, Dynamic UIM startup initialization, and context panel printing. Also notes `serverConnectionType` must not be changed.

children.title: Customizing the web application descriptor
Customizing the 404 or Page Not Found error response
children.summary: Explains how to customize the Cúram 404/Page Not Found response. The section notes that WebSphere and WebLogic default web.xml files define a default error page for HTTP 404 errors, and this message can be customized. It sits alongside guidance on web.xml customization, including separate approaches for development-time vs EAR packaging, preserving existing security/filter/servlet settings, and related HTTPS/default configuration behavior.

Explains how to customize the Cúram web application descriptor (web.xml) for development time, EAR packaging, or runtime-specific deployment, including where default files live for different application servers. Notes that existing security, filter, and servlet settings should not be changed, server/port settings are now auto-generated, locales can be added via ActionServlet init-params, and HTTPS enforcement can be relaxed by changing transport-guarantee. Also covers customizing the default 404/Page Not Found error response used by WebSphere and WebLogic.

Covers deployment-time web client configuration. Describes `ApplicationConfiguration.properties` location and copying the sample file for new apps, plus key settings for date/time, address defaults, uploads, synchronizer tokens, tab-session persistence, progress spinner, cache, Dynamic UIM init, stack trace output, DBtoJMS credentials, and context-panel printing; `serverConnectionType` must not be changed. Also explains web.xml customization for development, EAR packaging, or runtime, including default locations, locale additions, HTTPS transport-guarantee, and 404/page-not-found error page customization.

children.title: Customization
Adding new artifacts
children.summary: Explains how to add new artifacts to a Cúram web client customization component. New files are created directly in the custom component folder to extend the base application, with unique filenames to avoid overriding or merging existing artifacts. Notes that artifacts in the WebContent sub-folder always override delivered ones. Also briefly contrasts adding new artifacts with overriding/merging artifacts, where same-named files in higher-priority components replace or combine with lower-priority ones.

children.title: Overriding or merging artifacts
children.summary: Explains how Cúram web client customizations are applied through component order, without modifying base artifacts. New custom components should be added first in the component order for highest priority. The section distinguishes adding new artifacts from overriding or merging same-named artifacts: higher-priority components replace or combine lower-priority ones at build time. It notes that artifact files only need the same filename, not the same folder path, and that WebContent sub-folder artifacts always override delivered resources.

children.title: Externalized strings
children.summary: All string values in UIM files and JavaScript must be externalized for maintainability and localization. This section explains the properties-file format: name=value pairs, blank lines and # comments ignored, values preserved exactly, and Unicode escapes like \u00a0 for unsupported keyboard characters. It notes that dot-separated names are optional for structure. It also shows how custom components can override or add only selected properties without copying page/view files.

children.title: Images
children.summary: Explains how UIM image references are externalized via Image.properties files, similar to string properties. Image files belong in a component’s Images subfolder, can be localized with locale-suffixed properties files, and may be overridden in custom components. Recommends SVG for scalable graphics and notes browser support for SVG, PNG, GIF, and JPEG. Also covers image mapping for displaying field values as images, configured in ImageMapConfig.xml rather than UIM.

children.title: Image mapping
children.summary: Explains how images are externalized and referenced in UIMs via Image.properties, with keys mapped to relative image paths in a component’s Images folder. Notes support for SVG/PNG/GIF/JPEG, component overrides, and locale-specific Image.properties files for localized images. Also introduces image mapping in the application, where field values can be displayed as images instead of text (e.g., Boolean checkmark/X), with mappings defined in ImageMapConfig.xml rather than UIM.

children.title: The CuramLinks.properties file
children.summary: The CuramLinks.properties file maps UIM LINK page references (PAGE_ID_REF) to actual client page IDs, letting multiple links share one update point. Each component can supply its own file, and during generation the files are merged; if the same key appears in multiple components, the highest-priority component wins. This section also notes related runtime XML configuration files in <cdej-dir>/lib, which must be copied to a custom component subdirectory before modification and are selected by CLIENT_COMPONENT_ORDER.

children.title: Runtime configuration XML files
children.summary: This section explains runtime client XML configuration files in `<cdej-dir>/lib` and how to customize them by copying originals into a custom component subdirectory. It notes that client generators use the highest-priority version based on `CLIENT_COMPONENT_ORDER`. It also lists the runtime XML files involved: `CalendarConfig.xml`, `DynamicMenuConfig.xml`, `ICDynamicMenuConfig.xml`, `MeetingViewConfig.xml`, and `RatesTableConfig.xml`. It additionally covers merging and priority behavior for `ImageMapConfig.xml` and `CuramLinks.properties`.

children.title: Login Pages
children.summary: This section explains how to customize the default logon.jsp login page and related JavaScript. It notes the page location, how to override it in a custom component, required script includes to prevent dialog loading and set focus, and j_security_check form field requirements. It also covers styling via CSS and use of the UIM SCRIPT element for component-relative JavaScript paths. It warns that underlying HTML/CSS structure and unsupported JavaScript APIs may change across releases.

children.title: JavaScript files
children.summary: This section explains how to include custom JavaScript files in Cúram UI pages using the UIM SCRIPT element with a relative path preserved during generation. It warns that custom scripts depend on undocumented HTML/CSS structure and may break across releases. It also notes that only documented Cúram JavaScript APIs in CuramCDEJ\doc\Javascript\index.html are supported. The section briefly mentions login page customization, including required script inclusion to prevent dialog loading and the j_security_check form requirements.

children.title: CSS
children.summary: This section explains CSS customization in Cúram client pages. It states that default CSS files in WebContent/WEB-INF/css must not be modified; instead, create new CSS files in application components. Component CSS is automatically merged into custom.css and loaded on all client pages. The section warns that Cúram’s underlying HTML/CSS is undocumented and may change across releases, so customizations may need rework. It also gives examples of overriding existing selectors and extending them with more specific rules.

children.title: Application configuration files
children.summary: Explains where to place custom application configuration files for defining applications, sections, and tabs: in `<server-dir>\components\<component-name>\clientapps`, with subfolders allowed. Files there override same-named files in the component’s `tab` directory, which should not be modified. Notes that default Cúram configuration fragments are merged at build time, but this is not supported for custom artifacts, so no `tab` folder should exist in `EJBServer\components\custom`. Also says XML and properties files must be customized together as a unit.

children.title: General configuration
Customizing configuration settings
children.summary: Explains how to customize web client configuration by adding a curam-config.xml in a custom component, which is merged with the core file at generation time. Identifies global settings that must appear only once and are overridden by custom definitions (for example HELP, ERROR_PAGE, STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER) versus settings that are merged (for example MULTIPLE_POPUP_DOMAINS, PAGINATION, ADDRESS_CONFIG). Also notes that specific address formats can be overridden by matching name, with the custom definition taking precedence.

children.title: Dividing the configuration file
children.summary: Explains how to split curam-config.xml into smaller files by saving a fragment as a separate file ending in -config.xml (for example, address-config.xml) with the same APP_CONFIG root. Such files are merged at build time. Also distinguishes configuration naming patterns: XConfig.xml for standalone configs and X-config.xml for fragments of curam-config.xml. Ends by introducing mandatory Kubernetes deployment steps for configuring static content in Cúram, including the Citizen Portal.

children.title: STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER
Configuring static content for Cúram
children.summary: Explains how to configure static content for Cúram, a mandatory step for Kubernetes deployments. Set `STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER` in `curam-config.xml` with an absolute or relative static URL, then perform a full build. Use the `webclient` project’s `zip-static-content` target to create `StaticContent.zip` under `webclient/build` (optionally override with `-Dstatic.content.zip`). The zip stores content under `WebContent` and includes common HTML, image, font, script, and CSS file types.

children.title: Configuring static content for other Cúram applications
children.summary: Explains how to configure static content for other Cúram applications by setting the STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER in curam-config.xml/deployment, performing a full rebuild of server, client, and EAR files, and then building the external application client to gather static assets. It shows relocating static content to a separate HTTP server and notes that cache-control headers like Expires and Cache-control can be set to improve browser caching and performance. Includes a Citizen Portal build example.

Explains mandatory static-content setup for Kubernetes deployments. For Cúram, set STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER in curam-config.xml to an absolute or relative URL, run a full build, then package static assets with webclient’s zip-static-content target into StaticContent.zip under webclient/build (overridable via -Dstatic.content.zip). The zip stores WebContent files such as HTML, images, fonts, JS, CSS, JSON, and more. It also covers response headers like Expires and Cache-control for browser caching, plus a separate procedure for other Cúram applications, including full rebuild and Citizen Portal external-client generation.

children.title: POPUP_PAGES
children.summary: Defines popup-page related configuration entries and points to section 8.18 “Pop-up pages” for details. Also notes MULTIPLE_POPUP_DOMAINS uses the same reference. The excerpt transitions into ERROR_PAGE, which controls the page shown when a runtime error occurs, depending on error cause.

children.title: MULTIPLE_POPUP_DOMAINS
children.summary: MULTIPLE_POPUP_DOMAINS refers to the Pop-up pages configuration topic. This section does not define the setting itself here; it points to “8.18 Pop-up pages” on page 244 for details. Use this node if you need information about handling pop-up pages across multiple domains.

children.title: ERROR_PAGE
children.summary: Defines the runtime error page behavior controlled by the ERROR_PAGE setting. If an error occurs at run time, the user is redirected to the page specified by this setting. It notes that different error causes may use two kinds of error pages.

children.title: MULTIPLE_SELECT
children.summary: Defines the MULTIPLE_SELECT configuration section for forms. It specifies which domains should appear as multiple-select list boxes by setting `MULTIPLE="true"` on `<DOMAIN>` elements. Example given uses `PRIMARY_ID` and `OTHER_ID`. Useful if you need to configure or identify domains that allow multiple selections in UI forms.

children.title: FILE_DOWNLOAD_CONFIG
children.summary: FILE_DOWNLOAD_CONFIG is a placeholder section here with no descriptive text in this excerpt. It is referenced only by a cross-reference to ACTION_CONTROL for file download details. Nearby configuration topics on the page include ERROR_PAGE behavior, MULTIPLE_SELECT domain settings, ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS, APPEND_COLON, ADDRESS_CONFIG, and ADMIN.

children.title: ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS
children.summary: Controls whether UI clusters can be collapsed. It is enabled by default; set `<ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS>false</ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS>` to disable it. The section sits among other configuration options such as MULTIPLE_SELECT, APPEND_COLON, and ADDRESS_CONFIG, with an example showing the simple boolean element.

children.title: APPEND_COLON
children.summary: Controls whether colons are automatically appended to FIELD and CONTAINER labels inside CLUSTER elements. Set `APPEND_COLON` to `true` to enable this behavior. Example: `<APPEND_COLON>true</APPEND_COLON>`.

children.title: ADDRESS_CONFIG
children.summary: ADDRESS_CONFIG is a domain-specific control section. It refers readers to the “Domain-specific controls” chapter for details. In this excerpt, surrounding controls include ERROR_PAGE, MULTIPLE_SELECT, FILE_DOWNLOAD_CONFIG, ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS, APPEND_COLON, and ADMIN, but no ADDRESS_CONFIG-specific settings are shown here. Use this node if you need navigation to address-related configuration within the Cúram UI/configuration controls.

children.title: ADMIN
children.summary: ADMIN configures administrative updates in Cúram. It can contain any number of CODETABLE_UPDATE, TAB_CONFIG_UPDATE, and RESOURCE_UPDATE elements. This section follows settings for error pages, multiple select domains, file download config, collapsible clusters, colon appending, and address config. It is useful if you need to customize code tables, tabs, or resources at the admin level.

children.title: STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER
children.summary: Explains how to configure Cúram static content via the STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER setting in curam-config.XML, mainly for Kubernetes deployments. Describes using an absolute or relative URL, then performing a full build to apply it. Also covers packaging static assets with the webclient zip-static-content target, which creates StaticContent.zip (or a custom file via -Dstatic.content.zip) under webclient/build, storing content under WebContent. Includes the file types placed in the zip.

children.title: FIELD_ERROR_INDICATOR
children.summary: Describes the FIELD_ERROR_INDICATOR configuration option, which controls whether field-level error icons/tooltips appear for web-tier validation errors on text and date-time inputs. The alt text of the image shows the error message on hover. It does not apply to server-side exceptions. Example syntax is shown, and the default is FALSE if omitted. The page also begins the next setting, SECURITY_CHECK_ON_PAGE_LOAD.

children.title: SECURITY_CHECK_ON_PAGE_LOAD
children.summary: Explains the SECURITY_CHECK_ON_PAGE_LOAD setting: when a Cúram screen loads, all server functions on that page are authorization-checked up front. If the user lacks rights for any function, an authorization error occurs. The surrounding page also covers static content caching headers and the FIELD_ERROR_INDICATOR option for displaying field-level error icons/tooltips on web-tier validation messages.

children.title: ENABLE_SELECT_ALL_CHECKBOX
children.summary: Describes the ENABLE_SELECT_ALL_CHECKBOX configuration for multi-select list widgets. When set to true in curam-config.xml, it adds a header checkbox that selects or clears all row checkboxes at once. If omitted, the default is false. The section also notes the related MULTISELECT widget documentation.

children.title: TRANSFER_LISTS_MODE
children.summary: Describes the TRANSFER_LISTS_MODE configuration in curam-config.xml: when set to true, all multiple-selection controls in the application are displayed as Transfer List widgets. The default is false if unspecified. This section appears alongside related UI/security settings, including page-load authorization checks and the “select all” checkbox option.

children.title: HIDE_CONDITIONAL_LINKS
children.summary: Configures conditional-link visibility: HIDE_CONDITIONAL_LINKS defaults to true, hiding links whose conditions evaluate false; when false, such links are shown disabled. The excerpt also notes related UI settings: DISABLE_AUTO_COMPLETE defaults to false, SCROLLBAR_CONFIG can enable vertical scrollbars for LIST/CLUSTER after a max height, and PAGINATION controls list paging at the application level with a default-enabled setting and configurable page size/threshold.

children.title: DISABLE_AUTO_COMPLETE
children.summary: DISABLE_AUTO_COMPLETE controls whether browser autocomplete is enabled for all input fields. Set it to true to disable autocomplete, or false to enable it. If omitted, it defaults to FALSE.

children.title: SCROLLBAR_CONFIG
children.summary: Defines SCROLLBAR_CONFIG for application UI. It enables vertical scrollbars on LIST or CLUSTER elements once a maximum height is reached, using up to two ENABLE_SCROLLBARS entries. Each entry specifies TYPE (LIST or CLUSTER) and MAX_HEIGHT. Example shows LIST at 150 and CLUSTER at 100. If SCROLLBAR_CONFIG is omitted, no LIST or CLUSTER shows a vertical scrollbar.

children.title: PAGINATION
children.summary: Configures application-wide LIST pagination. Defines the PAGINATION element with ENABLED (default true) to turn paging on/off for lists. Example shows DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE and PAGINATION_THRESHOLD set to 15. When enabled, lists longer than the minimum size show only the first page and display pagination controls; individual lists can override the global settings.

General configuration for curam-config.xml: how core and custom component files are merged, with some global settings overriding and others merging (including address format overrides by matching name). Explains splitting config into -config.xml fragments and naming conventions. Covers mandatory Kubernetes static-content setup with STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER, full rebuild, zip-static-content packaging, file types, and caching headers, plus separate steps for other apps/Citizen Portal. Also documents popup pages, runtime ERROR_PAGE behavior, multiple-select domains, collapsible clusters, colon appending, admin updates, field error indicators, page-load security checks, select-all checkbox, transfer lists, conditional links, autocomplete, scrollbars, and pagination.

children.title: Custom resources
children.summary: Explains how to add custom files to the web application via a component’s WebContent folder. Warns that these files are copied directly into the generated WebContent root, can overwrite existing resources, and take precedence over normal resource merging/overriding (for example CSS and Struts config). Notes that files in WebContent are not processed, only copied, and that multiple components’ files are copied by component priority and timestamp. Includes basic steps for placing files and running the client build.

Explains how to customize a Cúram web client without changing base components, using component priority for new, overridden, or merged artifacts. Covers externalized strings, image references and locale-specific image properties, image mapping via ImageMapConfig.xml, CuramLinks.properties, and runtime XML config files. Also describes login page and JavaScript/CSS customization, application config placement, general curam-config.xml options (static content, security, pagination, UI behavior), and custom WebContent resources that are copied directly and can override generated output.

Explains the web client development environment: how CDEJ converts UIM files to JSPs, how client/server projects and install folders are organized, and how component-based customization works with priority and artifact types. Covers build output, locale handling, deployment/configuration files, and build/deploy workflows. Also includes runtime customization options such as static content hosting, security checks, UI behavior, pagination, scrollbars, and custom WebContent resource overrides.

children.title: 2.8 Deployment
Application configuration properties
Tracing server function calls
children.summary: This section explains how to enable tracing of server function calls in the web tier by setting `TraceOn=true` in `CDEJResources.properties`, which writes all call inputs and outputs to Standard Out. It also notes that the web tier does not have configurable Log4j 2 logging like the server tier. The remainder outlines how to customize `web.xml` for different environments and application servers, including development-time overrides, EAR packaging, and runtime customization via `custom-web-xml`.

Defines `ApplicationConfiguration.properties` for the web client and its required location/copy step. Lists configurable client settings for date/time formatting and separators, address defaults, upload limits and temp storage, synchronizer token behavior, tab session persistence thresholds, progress spinner enablement/threshold, and other options such as `serverConnectionType` (do not change), stack trace output for development only, DBtoJMS credentials, resource cache size, Dynamic UIM startup initialization, context panel print, and link-parameter sanitization.

children.title: Customizing the web application descriptor
Customizing the 404 or Page Not Found error response
children.summary: Explains how to customize the Cúram web client’s default 404/Page Not Found error page. It notes that WebSphere and WebLogic default web.xml files map HTTP 404 errors to a standard page, and that the displayed message can be changed by adding an HTTP404Error.properties file under <client-dir>/components/<component_name>/ and overriding error.message. Also emphasizes that web.xml customizations should preserve existing security, filter, and servlet settings.

Explains how to customize a Cúram web application’s web.xml descriptor, including changing server connection settings and session timeout. Lists the default web.xml locations for Development, WebSphere, WebLogic, and Liberty environments. Describes two customization paths: for development time, place a custom web.xml under a component’s WebContent/WEB-INF, with precedence determined by CLIENT_COMPONENT_ORDER; for EAR packaging, use deployment_packaging.xml in Server project/config and the custom-web-xml property. Mentions runtime customization is covered in the deployment guide.

Describes deployment-related web client configuration before installation. Covers how to copy and edit ApplicationConfiguration.properties and its main settings: date/time and address formats, upload limits and temp storage, synchronizer tokens, tab session persistence, progress spinner, serverConnectionType, stack traces, DBtoJMS credentials, cache size, Dynamic UIM startup, context panel printing, and link-parameter sanitization. Also explains tracing server calls via CDEJResources.properties. Ends with web.xml customization options for development and EAR packaging, default locations, precedence, and deployment_packaging.xml/custom-web-xml usage.

children.title: 2.9 Customization
Adding new artifacts
children.summary: Explains how to add new artifacts in a custom component. Create a new file in your component folder with a unique name; this extends the base application. Notes that all artifact types can be added this way, but files under WebContent always override delivered artifacts. Also sets up the distinction between adding new artifacts, overriding same-named artifacts, and merging artifacts during build-time selection based on component order.

children.title: Overriding or merging artifacts
children.summary: Explains how to customize Cúram applications using separate custom components with highest build-time priority. New artifacts can be added, and same-named artifacts may override or merge depending on type. UIM files are replaced by the highest-priority version; properties files are merged with key conflicts resolved in favor of the highest-priority component. Notes that custom components can be organized into subfolders, WebContent always overrides delivered artifacts, and build-time selection/merging does not modify original files.

children.title: Externalized strings
children.summary: Explains how externalized strings work in UIM and JavaScript: all string values must be moved to properties files for maintenance and localization. Describes properties file syntax (`name=value`), ignored blank/comment lines, Unicode escape sequences for unsupported characters, and that values are reproduced exactly in the final page. Notes that dot-separated names are optional but useful, and that custom components can override properties without copying the related page/view files. Includes links to help-content updates and JavaScript/UIM string references.

children.title: Images
children.summary: This section explains how UIM externalizes icons and graphics via Image.properties, mapping keys to image file paths relative to the component’s Images folder. It recommends SVG and notes browser support for SVG, PNG, GIF, and JPEG. It also covers overriding core images in custom components, locale-specific Image.properties files for localized images, and using images to represent field values through ImageMapConfig.xml rather than UIM.

children.title: Image mapping
children.summary: This section explains how Cúram externalizes and maps images. It covers the Image.properties file, where image keys are mapped to file paths under a component’s Images folder, using paths relative to the component. It recommends SVG and notes support for PNG, GIF, and JPEG. It also explains overriding core component images in custom components and localizing images with locale-specific Image.properties files. Finally, it introduces image mapping for displaying values as icons instead of text, with mappings defined in ImageMapConfig.xml rather than UIM.

children.title: The CuramLinks.properties file
children.summary: Explains the CuramLinks.properties file used by UIM LINK PAGE_ID_REF to indirectly map links to client pages. The file lets many links share one page reference, so updating a single entry changes all linked pages. Each component can define its own CuramLinks.properties, and during generation the files are merged by component priority; duplicate keys keep the highest-priority value. The section also briefly introduces runtime configuration XML files in <cdej-dir>/lib and notes that custom copies must be placed in the custom component subdirectory.

children.title: Runtime configuration XML files
children.summary: Explains runtime XML configuration files used by the running client application in `<cdej-dir>/lib`. To modify them, copy the original into the custom component subdirectory and edit the copy. Client generators use the XML file from the highest-priority component according to `CLIENT_COMPONENT_ORDER`. Lists the runtime files covered here: `CalendarConfig.xml`, `DynamicMenuConfig.xml`, and `ICDynamicMenuConfig.xml`.

children.title: Login Pages
children.summary: Explains how to customize the default `logon.jsp` login page by overriding it in a custom component, and the required JavaScript and form settings for proper login behavior (`no-dialog.jsp`, `Logon.js`, `ensureFullPageLogon()`, `j_security_check`, `j_username`, `j_password`). Also notes that styling can be changed via CSS, and that UIM SCRIPT elements can reference component-relative JavaScript files, but custom JavaScript may break across releases because HTML/CSS structure is not stable.

children.title: JavaScript files
children.summary: Explains how to use JavaScript in Cúram UIM pages via the SCRIPT element, specifying a relative path from the component folder (for example, `scripts/myScript.js`). Notes that file paths are preserved during application generation. Also warns that custom JavaScript depends on underlying HTML/CSS, which is undocumented and may change across Cúram releases, so scripts may need updates after upgrades.

children.title: CSS
children.summary: Explains Cúram CSS customization. Default CSS files must not be modified; instead add new CSS in application component folders, which is automatically concatenated into custom.css and loaded on all client pages. Warns that underlying HTML/CSS is undocumented and may change across releases. Shows overriding styles with more specific selectors, extending rules, using STYLE attributes for element-specific overrides, and BEM-style modifier classes. Also notes application-specific CSS via BODY classes and mentions browser-specific CSS, though cross-browser consistency is preferred.

children.title: Application configuration files
children.summary: Explains where to place custom application configuration files for Cúram web client apps: add them under `<server-dir>\components\<component-name>\clientapps`, which overrides same-named files in `tab`. Warns not to modify `tab` files or create a `tab` folder in custom components. Notes that app XML and properties files must be customized together, with matching root `id`, matching `.properties` filename, no periods/underscores in ids, and correct XML namespaces. Also introduces `curam-config.xml` as the main general web client configuration file that can be augmented or overridden.

children.title: General configuration
Customizing configuration settings
children.summary: Explains how to customize web client configuration files in a custom component. Covers placing application config artifacts in `<server-dir>\components\<component-name>\clientapps`, avoiding edits to default `tab` files, and customizing `.app`/`.properties` as a unit. Summarizes rules for IDs, namespaces, and property reuse. Also describes overriding and merging behavior for `curam-config.xml`: some settings are global and override core values, others are merged, with address format entries able to override specific formats.

children.title: Dividing the configuration file
children.summary: Explains how to split curam-config.xml into smaller configuration files. A partial config can be saved as a separate file ending in -config.xml (for example, address-config.xml) with the same APP_CONFIG root element, and it will be merged at build time. Also describes the two naming conventions: XConfig.xml for standalone config files and X-config.xml for fragments of curam-config.xml. Mentions related settings POPUP_PAGES, MULTIPLE_POPUP_DOMAINS, and ERROR_PAGE.

children.title: POPUP_PAGES
children.summary: This section lists configuration settings for pop-up pages and multiple pop-up domains, with references to the detailed “Pop-up pages” topic. It also introduces ERROR_PAGE, which controls the runtime redirect page shown on errors, supporting separate pages for system vs. application failures or one default page for both. The surrounding text explains configuration file naming and splitting curam-config.xml into merged -config.xml fragments.

children.title: MULTIPLE_POPUP_DOMAINS
children.summary: Defines configuration-file naming and splitting conventions for curam-config.xml, explaining how -config.xml files are merged at build time. Also notes popup-related settings: POPUP_PAGES and MULTIPLE_POPUP_DOMAINS both refer to the “Pop-up pages” section, and ERROR_PAGE controls which page users see on runtime errors, with options for system, application, or default error pages.

children.title: ERROR_PAGE
children.summary: Defines the ERROR_PAGE setting, which controls the page users are redirected to when a runtime error occurs. It notes that you can configure separate error pages for system failures and application failures, or a single default page for both.

children.title: MULTIPLE_SELECT
children.summary: Defines the MULTIPLE_SELECT configuration section for forms. It specifies which domains should appear as multiple-select list boxes using `DOMAIN` elements with `MULTIPLE="true"`. Example shown uses `PRIMARY_ID` and `OTHER_ID`. Relevant if you need to configure multi-selection behavior for domain fields.

children.title: FILE_DOWNLOAD_CONFIG
children.summary: Points to the FILE_DOWNLOAD_CONFIG section and notes that file download behavior is documented under the ACTION_CONTROL element on page 316. This node itself contains no configuration details beyond that cross-reference.

children.title: ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS
children.summary: ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS controls whether clusters can be collapsed in the UI. It defaults to true; set it to false to disable collapsible clusters. The section includes a simple XML example: `<ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS>false</ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS>`.

children.title: APPEND_COLON
children.summary: Controls whether FIELD and CONTAINER labels inside CLUSTER elements automatically end with colons. Set APPEND_COLON to true to append colons by default. Example shown: `<APPEND_COLON>true</APPEND_COLON>`.

children.title: ADDRESS_CONFIG
children.summary: ADDRESS_CONFIG is a domain-specific control section; this excerpt only points to “8 Domain-specific controls” for details. Nearby config topics include ERROR_PAGE override rules, MULTIPLE_SELECT for multi-select domain list boxes, FILE_DOWNLOAD_CONFIG reference, ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS (default true), APPEND_COLON for auto-appending colons to labels, and ADMIN updates. Useful if you need navigation into domain UI/control configuration, especially address-related settings.

children.title: ADMIN
children.summary: ADMIN configures administrative updates and can contain any number of CODETABLE_UPDATE, TAB_CONFIG_UPDATE, and RESOURCE_UPDATE elements. This page also covers nearby UI/configuration options such as ERROR_PAGE behavior (including TYPE-based overrides), MULTIPLE_SELECT domains, FILE_DOWNLOAD_CONFIG, ENABLE_COLLAPSIBLE_CLUSTERS, APPEND_COLON, and ADDRESS_CONFIG references. Useful if you need to manage admin-level component updates or related page/config settings.

children.title: STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER
children.summary: Explains how to configure Cúram static content delivery via the STATIC_CONTENT_SERVER element in curam-config.XML, including base and relative URL examples. Notes this is mandatory for Kubernetes deployments and requires a full Cúram build to take effect. Describes packaging static assets with the webclient zip-static-content target, which generates StaticContent.zip (default in webclient/build, overridable with -Dstatic.content.zip) under WebContent. Lists included file types such as .htc, .html/.htm, .bmp, .cur, .gif, .ico, and .jpeg.

children.title: FIELD_ERROR_INDICATOR
children.summary: Configures whether field-level error indicators appear for validation errors. When enabled, an image is shown for text input and date-time fields, with the error message as alt text and tooltip. It applies only to web-tier generated messages such as datatype validation or mandatory-field errors, not server-side exceptions. If not specified, the default is FALSE.

children.title: SECURITY_CHECK_ON_PAGE_LOAD
children.summary: Explains the SECURITY_CHECK_ON_PAGE_LOAD setting in curam-config.xml, which by default checks authorization for all server functions referenced by a UIM page before it loads.
