Its only attribute is optional SEPARATOR, which points to an externalized string used between parts. TITLE can contain one or more CONNECT child elements, but each CONNECT must include exactly one SOURCE. Sources may be server interface properties or, if NAME is TEXT, references to strings in a properties file.

children.title: VIEW element
children.summary: The VIEW element is the root element of a UIM document used to define elements for a UIM page document. It cannot include other views via INCLUDE and has no attributes. Its allowed child elements are PAGE_TITLE, SHORTCUT_TITLE, SERVER_INTERFACE, MENU, ACTION_SET, PAGE_PARAMETER, CONNECT, JSP_SCRIPTLET, CLUSTER, LIST, and SCRIPT.

Reference for PAGE/VIEW UIM structure and connection endpoints. Explains SOURCE/TARGET/INITIAL connection types, NAME values (server interface, TEXT, CONSTANT, JSCRIPT_REF, PAGE), and restrictions. Covers ACTION_CONTROL and SERVER_INTERFACE behavior, including submit/action phases, file downloads, clipboard/dismiss, and ACTION_ID_PROPERTY. Also defines PAGE_PARAMETER, PAGE_TITLE, TAB_NAME, TITLE, and SCRIPT, with their attributes and child CONNECT/DESCRIPTION rules. Useful for page-level wiring, titles, tab labels, and event scripting.

children.title: 13.7 UIM widgets reference
WIDGET element
children.summary: Defines the WIDGET element, which specifies a widget type and contains WIDGET_PARAMETER elements that configure it. Documents its attributes: required TYPE, plus optional LABEL, WIDTH, WIDTH_UNITS (PERCENT or CHARS, default PERCENT), HEIGHT (default 1), ALIGNMENT (LEFT/RIGHT/CENTER/DEFAULT, default DEFAULT), and HAS_CONFIRM_PAGE (MULTISELECT only, true/false, default false). Lists supported widget types such as FILE_UPLOAD, MULTISELECT, SINGLESELECT, FILE_DOWNLOAD, EVIDENCE_COMPARE, RULES_SIMULATION_EDITOR, FILE_EDIT, and IEG_PLAYER.

children.title: WIDGET_PARAMETER element
children.summary: Defines the WIDGET_PARAMETER element, used to configure an individual widget’s named properties and connect them to data endpoints. It has one required attribute, NAME, identifying the widget property being configured. It may contain CONNECT child elements, either multiple connections for a parameter (typically SOURCE, TARGET, INITIAL, depending on widget rules) or CONNECT pairs linking SOURCE to TARGET. Useful for understanding how widget properties are wired to data sources, targets, and initial values.

children.title: The FILE_EDIT widget
FILE_EDIT widget configuration
children.summary: Explains FILE_EDIT widget configuration options and related behavior during Word editing. Covers a setting to suppress duplicate Windows taskbar notifications for Word status messages, with an accessibility caution, and a logging setting that shows non-translated service messages in the File Edit Control dialog for troubleshooting. Also notes user computer configuration for the native messaging version of Word integration in Chrome/Edge Chromium, which relies on browser extensions and native messaging.

children.title: User computer configuration for the native messaging version
children.summary: Describes FILE_EDIT widget behavior and configuration for Microsoft Word editing in the browser, including user restrictions during editing, browser-close warnings, and persistence of interim saved changes. Summarizes settings in CDEJResources.properties such as fileedit.taskbar.messages (controls duplicated taskbar notifications, with accessibility implications) and fileedit.log.on (enables non-translated service log messages in the File Edit Control panel). Also notes that the native messaging version of Word integration for Google Chrome and Edge Chromium uses browser extension and native messaging features.

Describes the FILE_EDIT widget for editing Microsoft Word documents locally on Windows and saving them to the Cúram database. Covers supported browsers (Chrome/Edge Chromium via native messaging; IE unsupported), required parameters (DOCUMENT, DETAILS), template XML/UTF-8 constraints, editing session flow, commit/save behavior, browser-close warnings, and final save/landing-page handling. Also documents configuration settings: fileedit.taskbar.messages to suppress duplicate taskbar notifications (with accessibility caution) and fileedit.log.on to show non-translated service logs for troubleshooting.

children.title: The FILE_UPLOAD widget
children.summary: Describes the FILE_UPLOAD widget for selecting a local file to upload, usually shown as a text field plus browser-generated Browse button. Notes browser-dependent button appearance, locale, and width/security limitations. Explains server-side upload validation and temporary disk storage, with config settings uploadMaximumSize, uploadThresholdSize, and uploadRepositoryPath. Specifies required WIDGET type and parameters: CONTENT, optional FILE_NAME, CONTENT_TYPE, and ACCEPTABLE_CONTENT_TYPES, including their CONNECT requirements. Also warns about Chrome layout overlap with adjacent fields.

children.title: The FILE_DOWNLOAD widget
children.summary: Describes the FILE_DOWNLOAD widget, which generates a hyperlink that calls the Cúram FileDownload servlet to retrieve file contents from the database. Explains curam-config.xml setup, including server interface configuration, and notes that only one server interface can be configured per page. Compares FILE_DOWNLOAD widget vs ACTION_CONTROL for download links, especially fixed vs dynamic link text. Also covers use in Context Panel Actions menus and required widget parameters: LINK_TEXT (required) and PARAMS (optional, for passing page parameters to servlet).

children.title: The MULTISELECT widget
Confirmation Pages
children.summary: Explains confirmation pages for MULTISELECT widgets: set HAS_CONFIRM_PAGE="true" to send selections to a separate review page where users can confirm or go back. Large selections may require request forwarding due to URL length limits, so the confirmation URL shown may be the forwarding URL. The confirmation setting applies to the whole page, all submits. Also introduces SINGLESELECT, a radio-button list widget like MULTISELECT but limited to one selection and without pagination.

Explains the MULTISELECT widget for LISTs: it renders checkboxes in the first column to select multiple rows, with optional “Select All” in the header. Selected row data is compiled into a tab-separated string of “ | ”-delimited fields and passed to an action-phase target when submitted. Documents required/optional parameters: MULTI_SELECT_SOURCE, MULTI_SELECT_TARGET, MULTI_SELECT_INITIAL; up to three widget parameters. Notes HAS_CONFIRM_PAGE for confirmation-page submission and that MULTISELECT does not support pagination, so all items appear in one scrollable list.

children.title: The SINGLESELECT widget
children.summary: Describes the SINGLESELECT widget for LISTs: it renders radio buttons in the first column so users can choose exactly one row, behaving like MULTISELECT but with single-item selection. It does not support list pagination; all items appear in one scrollable list. The required SELECT_SOURCE parameter must contain multiple CONNECT elements with a SOURCE endpoint pointing to a list property that provides the row key data.

children.title: The IEG_PLAYER widget
children.summary: This section briefly describes the IEG_PLAYER widget and notes that further details are in the Authoring Scripts using Intelligent Evidence Gathering Guide. It then introduces Dynamic UIM: it is cached in the resource store, requires all string values to be externalized in properties files, and uses only PAGE as the valid root element. Most UIM features are supported except those listed as unsupported, and maintenance guidance is referenced elsewhere.

Reference for UIM widget elements. Defines WIDGET and WIDGET_PARAMETER, their attributes, and how widget properties connect to data endpoints. Covers FILE_EDIT (Word editing on Windows via Chrome/Edge Chromium, document/template requirements, save/commit flow, config notes), FILE_UPLOAD (local file selection, validation/config, browser quirks), FILE_DOWNLOAD (servlet-based file links, config and parameters), MULTISELECT (checkbox list selection, tab/pipe-delimited data, confirmation-page behavior, no pagination), SINGLESELECT (radio-button row selection, no pagination), and IEG_PLAYER/Dynamic UIM intro.

children.title: 13.8 Dynamic UIM
children.summary: Dynamic UIM pages are stored in the resource store instead of the file system, avoiding server/client rebuilds when pages change. All string values must be externalized into property files, which are also cached in the resource store. A dynamic UIM document may use only PAGE as the root element. Most UIM page/view features are supported, except those listed in the unsupported-features section, and maintenance details are referenced in the dynamic UIM pages chapter.

children.title: 13.9 Unsupported features in dynamic UIM
Unsupported ACTION_CONTROL features
children.summary: This section states that some ACTION_CONTROL features are not supported in dynamic UIM. It appears within the dynamic UIM limitations list, where static UIM support is referenced instead. The excerpt shown does not enumerate the unsupported ACTION_CONTROL features here, so this node mainly signals a restriction category rather than specific details.

children.title: Unsupported ACTION_SET features
children.summary: Lists unsupported ACTION_SET features in dynamic UIM. It says CONDITION and SEPARATOR child elements are not supported, and TOP and BOTTOM attributes are not supported. The section points readers to the full ACTION_SET documentation on page 320 for supported static UIM behavior.

children.title: Unsupported CLUSTER features
children.summary: This section lists static UIM features that are not supported in dynamic UIM. For CLUSTER, unsupported items include TITLE, DESCRIPTION, WIDGET child elements, and SUMMARY and TAB_ORDER attributes, with a note that dynamic conditional clusters are also unsupported. It also identifies unsupported CONTAINER features (IMAGE child element, LABEL_ABBREVIATION attribute) and FIELD features (LABEL and SCRIPT child elements). Useful for checking whether a UIM page uses unsupported dynamic elements.

children.title: Unsupported CONTAINER features
children.summary: Lists CONTAINER features not supported in dynamic UIM. Specifically, the IMAGE child element and LABEL_ABBREVIATION attribute are unsupported. It also cross-references the static UIM CONTAINER element for supported features.

children.title: Unsupported FIELD features
children.summary: This section lists FIELD features that are not supported in dynamic UIM pages. Specifically, the unsupported FIELD child elements are LABEL and SCRIPT. It also points readers to the FIELD element documentation for the full list of supported static UIM features.

children.title: Unsupported INFORMATIONAL features
children.summary: This section lists dynamic UIM features that are not supported. For INFORMATIONAL, only informational messages whose connection endpoints are associated with a server interface defined in the DISPLAY phase are supported; other endpoint types are not. For INLINE_PAGE, the unsupported attributes are URI_SOURCE_NAME and URI_SOURCE_PROPERTY. For LINK, the unsupported items are the CONDITION child element and PAGE_ID_REF attribute.

children.title: Unsupported INLINE_PAGE features
children.summary: This section lists INLINE_PAGE features not supported in dynamic UIM. Specifically, the URI_SOURCE_NAME and URI_SOURCE_PROPERTY attributes are unsupported. It also points readers to the static UIM INLINE_PAGE documentation for full supported-feature details. Useful if you need to verify whether an INLINE_PAGE configuration relies on URI-based source attributes.

children.title: Unsupported LINK features
children.summary: Dynamic UIM does not support certain LINK features. Specifically, the unsupported LINK items are the CONDITION child element and the PAGE_ID_REF attribute. The section also notes that supported LINK behavior in static UIM is documented elsewhere.

children.title: Unsupported LIST features
children.summary: This section lists LIST features not supported in dynamic UIM. It identifies unsupported child elements TITLE, DESCRIPTION, FOOTER_ROW, and ACTION_CONTROL, and unsupported attributes SUMMARY, SORTABLE, PAGINATED, DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE, and PAGINATION_THRESHOLD. It also introduces the next topic, unsupported MENU features, with a note that supported static UIM details are covered elsewhere.

children.title: Unsupported MENU features
children.summary: This section lists MENU features that are not supported in dynamic UIM. It appears to be part of a broader reference on unsupported UI element features, following a similar LIST features table. Useful if you need to check which MENU attributes or child elements are unavailable in dynamic UIM and to compare with the static UIM MENU documentation.

children.title: Unsupported PAGE features
children.summary: This section lists PAGE features not supported in dynamic UIM. Unsupported child elements include FIELD, CONTAINER, WIDGET, INCLUDE, SHORTCUT_TITLE, TAB_NAME, JSP_SCRIPTLET, and SCRIPT. Unsupported attributes include SCRIPT_FILE, POPUP_PAGE, APPEND_COLON, HIDE_CONDITIONAL_LINKS, COMPONENT_STYLE, and TYPE. It also notes that PAGE_TITLE features are unsupported, with static UIM support details referenced elsewhere.

children.title: Unsupported PAGE_TITLE features
children.summary: This section states which PAGE_TITLE features are not supported in dynamic UIM, with a pointer to the static UIM PAGE_TITLE documentation for supported details. It appears as part of a broader unsupported-features list on the same page, alongside unsupported PAGE and MENU items. Useful if you need to check feature compatibility for PAGE_TITLE, but the excerpt does not list the specific unsupported PAGE_TITLE attributes or child elements.

children.title: Unsupported SERVER_INTERFACE features
children.summary: This section lists dynamic UIM limitations for two elements. For SERVER_INTERFACE, the unsupported feature is the ACTION_ID_PROPERTY attribute, with a reference to the static UIM documentation for supported behavior. For WIDGET, several attributes are unsupported: WIDTH, WIDTH_UNITS, ALIGNMENT, HAS_CONFIRM_PAGE, CONFIG, and COMPONENT_STYLE. It also notes that for the TYPE attribute, only SINGLESELECT and MULTISELECT are supported; all other values are unsupported.

children.title: Unsupported WIDGET features
children.summary: This section lists WIDGET features unsupported in dynamic UIM. It identifies unsupported attributes WIDTH, WIDTH_UNITS, ALIGNMENT, HAS_CONFIRM_PAGE, CONFIG, and COMPONENT_STYLE, plus the TYPE attribute with only SINGLESELECT and MULTISELECT supported; all other TYPE values are unsupported. Useful for checking whether a WIDGET definition relies on static-UIM-only properties.

Lists dynamic UIM restrictions and unsupported elements/attributes. Covered areas include ACTION_CONTROL, ACTION_SET, CLUSTER, CONTAINER, FIELD, INFORMATIONAL, INLINE_PAGE, LINK, LIST, MENU, PAGE, PAGE_TITLE, SERVER_INTERFACE, and WIDGET. Key limits: dynamic UIM must use PAGE as root and all strings externalized; unsupported items include child elements like CONDITION, TITLE, DESCRIPTION, WIDGET, FIELD, CONTAINER, and attributes like URI_SOURCE_NAME, PAGE_ID_REF, PAGINATED, MODE (only IN_PAGE_NAVIGATION), ACTION_ID_PROPERTY, and several WIDGET sizing/configuration attributes. Useful for checking static-to-dynamic UIM compatibility.

children.title: 13.10 Dynamic UIM system initialization
children.summary: Explains Dynamic UIM system startup behavior. By default, the system initializes when the application starts. To defer initialization until the first request for a Dynamic UIM page, add `dynamicUIMInitModelOnStart=false` to `ApplicationConfiguration.properties`. Use the default startup initialization if the application uses dynamic UIM pages; override it only if dynamic UIM is unused and Tomcat startup is too slow.

Reference for Cúram UIM metadata: XML used to define web client pages, translated by CDEJ into JSP, with schema support for validation/editor help. Covers PAGE and VIEW document types, page structure/order rules, view inclusion constraints, field validation display, and mandatory externalized strings. The main reference details connections, ACTION_CONTROLs, file downloads, page/view elements, titles, tabs, scripts, and widget definitions. Also covers Dynamic UIM: resource-store pages, required externalized strings, unsupported elements/attributes, and system initialization behavior.
